commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `--enable-commands-exact`, exact command matching helpers/tests, and wires the new flag into runtime allowlist enforcement while preserving prefix matching for `--enable-commands`.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of the PR tree shows the added `EnableCommandsExact` flag has no default/env wiring while existing command guards do; no repo-mutating runtime test was run during this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the remaining blocker combines a security-boundary configuration concern with contributor-owned real behavior proof.

target.title: fix: add exact command allowlist matching
target.body:
## Summary

Adds `--enable-commands-exact` for strict command allowlisting.

The existing `--enable-commands` behavior remains unchanged and continues to allow command-prefix matches. This preserves compatibility for users who intentionally allow whole command families such as `gmail` or `drive`.

The new exact allowlist mode lets agent and CI callers permit only specific command paths, such as `gmail.search`, without also enabling sibling commands such as `gmail.send`.

## Security impact

Before this change, command allowlist matching was prefix-based. A broad allowlist entry such as `gmail` enabled the full Gmail command family. That is useful for humans, but risky for agent deployments because the Gmail family includes read, write, and settings/admin-style commands.

This change provides a non-breaking strict alternative:

- `--enable-commands gmail` keeps the existing prefix behavior.
- `--enable-commands-exact gmail.search` allows only `gmail search`.
- `--enable-commands-exact gmail.search` blocks `gmail send`.

## Behavior proof

### Unit proof

Command:

```text
go test ./internal/cmd -run 'TestParseEnabledCommands|TestCommandPathMatches|TestCommandPathMatchesExact|TestCommandPathMatchesExactAll' -count=1
```

Expected result:

```text
PASS
ok  	github.com/steipete/gogcli/internal/cmd
```

### Real CLI block proof

Command:

```text
go build -o /tmp/gogcli-exact-proof ./cmd/gog

/tmp/gogcli-exact-proof \
  --enable-commands-exact gmail.search \
  gmail send \
  --to proof@example.com \
  --subject proof \
  --body proof \
  --dry-run
```

Expected result:

```text
command "gmail send" is not enabled (set --enable-commands or --enable-commands-exact to allow it)
```

### Real CLI allow proof

Command:

```text
/tmp/gogcli-exact-proof \
  --enable-commands-exact gmail.search \
  gmail search test \
  --max 1
```

Expected result:

The command is not blocked by the exact allowlist gate. If no account is configured locally, it can fail later with normal account/auth handling. That still proves `gmail search` passed exact command gating while `gmail send` did not.

## Compatibility

This is non-breaking. Existing `--enable-commands` prefix behavior is unchanged.

## Note

This branch was prepared through the GitHub API, so it contains several small mechanical commits. The final diff is limited to `internal/cmd/enabled_commands.go`, `internal/cmd/enabled_commands_test.go`, and `internal/cmd/root.go` and can be squash-merged into one commit.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
